masthead.headline: Investigaciones
masthead.subtitle: Data analysis and investigative reporting on 23 years of Mexican federal procurement

featured.label: FEATURED STORY
featured.statSuffix: direct awards in 2023 — highest year in 23 years

methodology.title: Methodology & Data
methodology.description: All investigations are based on 3,051,294 federal contracts (2002–2025) from COMPRANET. Risk scoring uses logistic regression trained on 748 documented corruption cases. All data, methodology and code are open source.
methodology.tooltipBody: Logistic regression with 9 active features, 13 sector sub-models, AUC=0.828. Trained with 748 documented corruption cases. PU-learning (Elkan & Noto 2008).

la-cuarta-adjudicacion.headline: The Fourth Direct Award
la-cuarta-adjudicacion.subheadline: How the AMLO government drove no-bid contracts to a 23-year high — while promising to end corruption.

el-granero-vacio.headline: The Empty Granary: Segalmex
el-granero-vacio.subheadline: The story of the largest fraud in federal food distribution history — $15 billion, zero competitive tenders.
el-granero-vacio.statLabel: billion MXN in food distribution fraud

los-nuevos-ricos-de-la-4t.headline: The 4T's New Rich
los-nuevos-ricos-de-la-4t.subheadline: 1,253 ghost companies created after 2018 that accumulated government contracts without competition.

hemoser-el-2-de-agosto.headline: HEMOSER: August 2nd
hemoser-el-2-de-agosto.subheadline: 17.2 billion pesos in 12 contracts — all awarded in a single day to a single blood-products supplier.
hemoser-el-2-de-agosto.statLabel: billion MXN in 12 contracts, one day

la-austeridad-que-no-fue.headline: The Austerity That Wasn't
la-austeridad-que-no-fue.subheadline: In the middle of austerity rhetoric, 80% of federal contracts went without competitive tender in 2021.

cero-competencia.headline: Zero Competition
cero-competencia.subheadline: Half a million tenders where only one bidder showed up. The competition that never arrived.

triangulo-farmaceutico.headline: The Pharmaceutical Triangle
triangulo-farmaceutico.subheadline: Three suppliers that divided 270 billion pesos in health sector contracts across two administrations.

avalancha-diciembre.headline: The December Avalanche
avalancha-diciembre.subheadline: Every year, the government spends more in December than any other month. In 2015: 57.5 billion pesos in 31 days.

cartel-del-corazon.headline: The Cardiac Cartel
cartel-del-corazon.subheadline: One company dominated cardiac equipment procurement for over a decade — until COFECE launched an investigation.

infraestructura-sin-competencia.headline: Infrastructure Without Competition
infraestructura-sin-competencia.subheadline: 2.1 trillion pesos in infrastructure contracts with only one proposal submitted. The bidder was always the same.

la-casa-de-los-contratos.headline: The House of Contracts
la-casa-de-los-contratos.subheadline: The network of companies linked to Grupo Higa and the mega infrastructure project that defined the Peña Nieto era.

oceanografia.headline: Oceanografía: Two Frontiers
oceanografia.subheadline: The fraud that crossed from PEMEX to Banamex to Citibank. Fake invoices at international scale.

sixsigma-hacienda.headline: SixSigma and the SAT
sixsigma-hacienda.subheadline: How a technology company manipulated the tax authority's tenders for years — and the data trail it left.

sexenio-a-sexenio.headline: Administration by Administration
sexenio-a-sexenio.subheadline: From 63% to 82%: how direct awards grew without pause across 23 years, regardless of who was in power.

red-fantasma.headline: Ghost Network: Anatomy of a Non-Existent Company
red-fantasma.subheadline: SAT confirmed 13,960 companies that invoiced simulated operations to the government. Here is how they work.
red-fantasma.statLabel: companies on SAT's EFOS list — confirmed shell operations

el-ano-sin-excusas.headline: 2023: The Year Mexico Broke Every Record
el-ano-sin-excusas.subheadline: The last full year of the administration recorded the highest rate of no-bid contracts in the country's modern history: 81.9%.

insabi-el-experimento.headline: INSABI: The Experiment That Collapsed Supply
insabi-el-experimento.subheadline: Dissolving the Seguro Popular dismantled competition mechanisms in drug procurement, sending direct awards to 94%.
insabi-el-experimento.statLabel: direct awards in INSABI drug procurement

tren-maya-sin-reglas.headline: Tren Maya: $180 Billion Without a Single Tender
tren-maya-sin-reglas.subheadline: Mexico's most expensive infrastructure project bypassed procurement rules through emergency declarations and direct contracts to inexperienced companies.

fabrica-de-monopolios.headline: The Monopoly Factory
fabrica-de-monopolios.subheadline: In energy and technology, 10% of suppliers took over 70% of the budget. The AMLO model repeated the PRI pattern — at greater scale.
fabrica-de-monopolios.statLabel: of the budget to 10% of suppliers

el-dinero-de-todos.headline: Everyone's Money: How Procurement Concentrated in Few Hands
el-dinero-de-todos.subheadline: Over two decades, Mexican federal spending shifted from broad competition to oligopoly. RUBLI's analysis of 3.1 million contracts reveals who benefits.

pandemia-sin-supervision.headline: Pandemic Without Supervision
pandemia-sin-supervision.subheadline: Mexico spent over 40 billion in COVID procurement without tenders. 73% went to companies created less than two years before receiving their contract.

pemex-el-gigante.headline: PEMEX Never Competes: The $2 Trillion Black Hole
pemex-el-gigante.subheadline: PEMEX and CFE account for 40% of federal procurement but less than 5% is competitive. Twenty years of data show the pattern predated AMLO — and outlasted him.

atlas-del-riesgo.headline: Risk Atlas: The Sectors Where Corruption Leaves the Most Traces
atlas-del-riesgo.subheadline: RUBLI's model identifies 184,031 contracts with critical-level risk signals. Health and agriculture lead in risk rate; infrastructure leads in amount.
atlas-del-riesgo.statLabel: contracts at critical risk level (v6.5, threshold ≥0.60)

la-herencia-envenenada.headline: The Poisoned Legacy: What AMLO Left in Public Finances
la-herencia-envenenada.subheadline: The administration ended with historic direct-award rates, 247,946 contracts suspected of threshold-splitting, and a transparency debt that will take years to resolve.
la-herencia-envenenada.statLabel: contracts put to a single bidder

dividir-para-evadir.headline: Divide to Evade: 247,946 Contracts Designed to Dodge Scrutiny
dividir-para-evadir.subheadline: The law requires public tendering above certain thresholds. RUBLI found 247,946 contracts clustered just below those limits — statistically improbable by chance.

la-maquina-de-papel.headline: The Paper Machine
la-maquina-de-papel.subheadline: COMPRANET was created in 1996 to make procurement transparent. What it became was a system where the rules of competition could be observed — and gamed — simultaneously.
la-maquina-de-papel.statLabel: years of procurement data in COMPRANET
